item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Upvoted. It's not mind-blowing, but I like it, even if the Phobos Labs thing doesn't seem that original - this site already has a lot of corporations that make SCP-type stuff while still using typical customer service tropes. The idea isn't bad, it's just the execution, in the form of the letter's format, that's kind of bland and cliché. This can easily be fixed, though, if you want to.

Despite possessing a fully developed reproductive system, this lentivirus acts as SCP-2850’s means of reproduction.

I suggest: "Despite the fact that SCP-2850 possesses a normal, fully developed reproductive system, it only reproduces by means of this lentivirus."

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like the concept behind the GoI. The problem I see is one of marketing; where are they getting clientele rich enough to afford custom nightmares? Why would you sell something that could become a global plague? Without a built-in terminus, these things would spread like crazy. Products that come back to haunt/kill you don't sell very well after the first failure.— And what would Marshall, Carter and Dark (an established group of rich psycho businessmen) have to say about the competition??

Overall the piece itself doesn't do it for me. The containment is somewhat vague, and the concept of sacrificing a D-Class so it can reproduce merely to contain it conflicts with current standards. If these will die out once contained, why wouldn't we let them?

The process of reproduction is very well written, with accurate (from my PoV) use of technical terms. Maybe it's just my cynical nature combined with desensitization from reading this site for years, but I was able to see the placements of elements for a squick factor. It killed my immersion just as a microphone dropping into a shot does for TV.

As always, these are just my feelings and issues. I'd like to see more of Phobos Labs. No vote.

item.title: UAC?
item.content:encoded:
Phobos Labs

This isn't Doom! I do think it's still a fitting name for the company responsible for SCP-2850, considering its purpose is manufacturing incarnations of fear.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like this, but I can't bring myself to upvote based on the points mentioned above. I'd like to see Phobos expanded upon, though.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That was one of the similarities I noticed along with them both being [Word that starts with P] Labs.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I found it interesting that Phobos and Prometheus both are related to Greek Mythology. Phobos is the personification of fear, the offspring of Aphrodite and Ares/Mars (and the planet Mars has two moons, Phobos and Deimos). Prometheus is a titan who brought fire to mankind and in some versions of the myth, shaped the first humans from mud.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: They are not. Phobos is Greek for "fear", which I felt appropriate given their objectives.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Is Phobos Labs supposed to be related to Prometheus Labs somehow? Their names are very similar.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Eh, I'll stick with a neutral vote. While I really like some of the things here, I feel that it is (probably non-intentionally) a compilation of a bunch of already existing "scary stuff" without actually adding much to make it truly stand out. Hope to see more developed concepts from you in the future.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like how generic this feels. A company 'standardizing' abject horror would probably end up like this. It's not super-good at it but it's consistent. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I would be careful about pushing a new (or any) GoI to hard in your articles. GoI related skips or trying to introduce new GoIs seems to be pretty polarizing in the community from what I've seen. I personally don't mind, and actually enjoy, new and existing GoIs if done right.

I do like the idea of a company that makes "customized nightmares", and I do like this skip. "Squirming" is just the perfect word to use there, kudos for upsetting me. I would like to see more Phobo Labs, as long as they're not pushed to hard. I like the skip and would like to see it stay. Up vote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I kind of am more interested in the TrypanoClowns, to be 100% honest.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I definitely like Phobos Labs at the moment, but I'm considering scrapping this article for something that focuses a little more heavily on the GOI.

That said, my idea behind the group was less "creating weaponized SCPs" and more "creating customized nightmares." Both objects I referenced here were common fears/themes of nightmares (insects, teeth falling out/rotting, clowns, needles) and I like the idea of a GoI that creates customized nightmares for their clientele.

item.title: Neutral overall
item.content:encoded:
I don't care for a lab that's only existence is to do creepy shit. That's kinda lame.

I want to upvote for the squirming sensation note, though.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
For me this is a potentially interesting new group of interest with a well done vanilla SCP attached. +1 for Phobos Labs more than the tooth wasps, because I like the idea of a company dedicated to creating weaponized SCP's for the 3rd world dictator on a budget.

Edit: almost strikes me that some of what Kalinin points out could actually be built into the heart of this company's lore- something like they take well understood or common effects present in many SCP's, and reverse engineer them to build streamlined, easy to contain and target versions.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Now, see, my first thoughts were "Tooth Fairies" as well, but the idea of a company existing solely to sell nightmares or the contents of nightmares incarnate is what separates this. For me, at least.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This article does a lot of things, but none that are particularly outstanding or better than previous forays into the realms of tooth horror, insect-driven body horror, or GoIs that deploy nightmarish clownfolk. More than anything, this feels like a somewhat artificial attempt to push a new GoI.

Generally speaking, it's not bad, but it's not really memorable to me in any way either, and I don't care for things with no single outstanding attribute continuing to exist on the mainlist. -1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: YAY! A new group of interest!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Fair enough; thanks for the input!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks! I would like to write more from Phobos Labs and am brainstorming.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This. It's well done, but it's been done better elsewhere.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Enh, I feel like that note at the end was a little too on-the-nose. Also, I think the Tooth Fairies did the whole "too many teeth" thing better. And I could swear that there's an SCP that does the "spewing insects from your mouth" bit, but I can't remember off hand.

Overall, it's well written and executed, but I don't care for the core concept. Downvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I want to see more from Phobos Labs.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like it. It is well written, concise (something I struggle with) and a bit creepy. It feels a lot like Series 1 or 2 articles but I don't mean that in a bad way.
